description: In 'sulopa', liquid carries or surrounds the other ingredients of a prepared food. Soup may be thin or thick, hot or cold, and served as a portion or a shared dish. Drinking defines a drink; soup remains food even when someone sips it.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced 's' pass along the centre of the tongue into high-back rounded 'u'. Touch the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and move into stressed rounded mid-back 'o', then close both lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into open 'a'.
usage_notes: Broth may be called soup when it is treated as the dish itself. Ingredients, preparation method, temperature, and cultural name can be stated separately.

semantic_domains.activity: a dish prepared, served, and eaten as food
semantic_domains.physical: food whose ingredients are held in a liquid base
